groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Minimum information required to understand the app, its audience, its value, and the intended store destination.

groups.title: Audience & Positioning
groups.description: Market position, audience problem, differentiation, voice, and geographic relevance.

groups.title: Product & Trust Details
groups.description: Functionality, pricing, requirements, supported devices, evidence, privacy information, and visual assets.

groups.title: Discovery, Visuals & Release
groups.description: Keyword direction, promotional messaging, listing rewrites, screenshot concepts, and version-update content.

groups.title: Compliance & Output
groups.description: Sensitive categories, age considerations, wording restrictions, requested output modules, and final requirements.

variables.label: Store destination
variables.placeholder: Select the store destination
variables.hint: Choose whether the listing is for Apple, Google Play, or both.

help.what: Provide the store destination for this app store listing.
help.why: Store rules, metadata limits, and output sections differ by destination.
help.example: Good example: Apple App Store
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, future features, invented claims, or benefits the current app does not support.

variables.label: App name
variables.placeholder: Creatornivo
variables.hint: Enter the public app name exactly as it should appear.

help.what: Provide the app name for this app store listing.
help.why: The app name anchors metadata, title options, and review checks.
help.example: Good example: Creatornivo
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, future features, invented claims, or benefits the current app does not support.

variables.label: App category
variables.placeholder: Select app category
variables.hint: Choose the closest store category or leave Auto.

help.what: Optional context for the app category field.
help.why: Category helps shape keywords, positioning, and compliance expectations.
help.example: Good example: Productivity
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: What does the app do?
variables.placeholder: A structured AI content workspace that turns briefs into platform-ready drafts.
variables.hint: Explain the app in plain factual language.

help.what: Provide the what does the app do? for this app store listing.
help.why: The listing cannot be accurate without a clear description of the current app.
help.example: Good example: A structured AI content workspace that turns briefs into platform-ready drafts.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, future features, invented claims, or benefits the current app does not support.

variables.label: Target users
variables.placeholder: Solo founders and creators who publish content every week.
variables.hint: Describe the users this app is built for.

help.what: Provide the target users for this app store listing.
help.why: Audience context controls benefits, screenshots, keywords, and tone.
help.example: Good example: Solo founders and creators who publish content every week.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, future features, invented claims, or benefits the current app does not support.

variables.label: Core features
variables.placeholder: Template library, saved prompts, personal content library, Markdown export.
variables.hint: List the real features users can access now.

help.what: Provide the core features for this app store listing.
help.why: The prompt must not invent app functionality or imply unavailable features.
help.example: Good example: Template library, saved prompts, personal content library, Markdown export.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, future features, invented claims, or benefits the current app does not support.

variables.label: Key user benefits
variables.placeholder: Create consistent first drafts without rebuilding every prompt from scratch.
variables.hint: Explain the practical value users receive from those features.

help.what: Provide the key user benefits for this app store listing.
help.why: Benefits connect features to user outcomes while staying truthful.
help.example: Good example: Create consistent first drafts without rebuilding every prompt from scratch.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, future features, invented claims, or benefits the current app does not support.

variables.label: Listing goal
variables.placeholder: Select listing goal
variables.hint: Choose the main goal for this listing package.

help.what: Optional context for the listing goal field.
help.why: The goal affects emphasis across metadata, screenshots, and review notes.
help.example: Good example: Drive qualified installs
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Monetization model
variables.placeholder: Select monetization model
variables.hint: Choose how the app is currently monetized.

help.what: Optional context for the monetization model field.
help.why: Pricing language and disclosure depend on the monetization model.
help.example: Good example: Freemium
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Listing stage
variables.placeholder: Select listing stage
variables.hint: Choose whether this is a new listing, refresh, or version update.

help.what: Optional context for the listing stage field.
help.why: The stage determines whether existing copy or release-note fields matter.
help.example: Good example: New launch
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: English
variables.hint: Enter the language or regional variant for the listing.

help.what: Provide the output language for this app store listing.
help.why: The copy should match the language intended for the store listing.
help.example: Good example: English
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, future features, invented claims, or benefits the current app does not support.

variables.label: User problem or need
variables.placeholder: Creators waste time rewriting prompts and lose consistency across channels.
variables.hint: Describe the problem that makes the app useful.

help.what: Optional context for the user problem or need field.
help.why: Problem context improves relevance without relying on generic benefits.
help.example: Good example: Creators waste time rewriting prompts and lose consistency across channels.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: What makes it different?
variables.placeholder: Template-first workflow, transparent generation limits, and no fake social proof.
variables.hint: Explain factual differences from alternatives.

help.what: Optional context for the what makes it different? field.
help.why: Differentiation helps positioning while avoiding unsupported superiority claims.
help.example: Good example: Template-first workflow, transparent generation limits, and no fake social proof.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Alternative solutions
variables.placeholder: Alternative to manually rebuilding prompts in a general AI chat tool.
variables.hint: Describe relevant alternatives or comparison context.

help.what: Optional context for the alternative solutions field.
help.why: It helps keep comparisons fair and specific.
help.example: Good example: Alternative to manually rebuilding prompts in a general AI chat tool.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Brand voice
variables.placeholder: Select brand voice
variables.hint: Choose the tone that matches the app and audience.

help.what: Optional context for the brand voice field.
help.why: Voice affects listing copy, screenshot captions, and review notes.
help.example: Good example: Clear and trustworthy
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Target countries or regions
variables.placeholder: United States, United Kingdom, Canada
variables.hint: Add markets where language, terminology, or compliance context matters.

help.what: Optional context for the target countries or regions field.
help.why: Regional context can change spelling, examples, claims, and metadata choices.
help.example: Good example: United States, United Kingdom, Canada
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Feature details
variables.placeholder: Includes template-specific forms, prompt saving, generated content saving, and export.
variables.hint: Add deeper product details that should appear in the listing.

help.what: Optional context for the feature details field.
help.why: Detailed facts make the output more concrete without adding unsupported claims.
help.example: Good example: Includes template-specific forms, prompt saving, generated content saving, and export.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Pricing and purchases
variables.placeholder: Free plan with daily limits; Pro monthly plan for higher usage.
variables.hint: Describe pricing, subscriptions, purchases, or free access.

help.what: Optional context for the pricing and purchases field.
help.why: Commercial details must be accurate and not implied from the monetization model alone.
help.example: Good example: Free plan with daily limits; Pro monthly plan for higher usage.
help.avoid: Avoid approximate prices, hidden conditions, or purchase claims that are not confirmed.

variables.label: Supported devices
variables.placeholder: Select supported devices
variables.hint: List the devices or platforms the app supports. Current form controls do not support checkboxes for this template yet, so list the relevant options separated by commas.

help.what: List one or more relevant options separated by commas. This preserves the prompt variable while using the current textarea control.
help.why: Device support affects Apple and Google Play copy and review checks.
help.example: Good example: iPhone, iPad, Android phone
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Usage requirements
variables.placeholder: Requires an account and internet connection.
variables.hint: List login, subscription, hardware, OS, or connectivity requirements.

help.what: Optional context for the usage requirements field.
help.why: Users and reviewers need to know material requirements before installing.
help.example: Good example: Requires an account and internet connection.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Approved proof or credentials
variables.placeholder: Use only approved facts; no awards or rankings yet.
variables.hint: Add approved awards, certifications, ratings, credentials, or proof.

help.what: Optional context for the approved proof or credentials field.
help.why: Proof can support trust only when it is real and approved.
help.example: Good example: Use only approved facts; no awards or rankings yet.
help.avoid: Avoid invented awards, ratings, rankings, certifications, user counts, or review claims.

variables.label: Privacy and data use
variables.placeholder: User content is saved in a personal library when the user chooses.
variables.hint: Describe relevant data collection, account, privacy, or security notes.

help.what: Optional context for the privacy and data use field.
help.why: Privacy-related copy must stay accurate and avoid unsupported assurances.
help.example: Good example: User content is saved in a personal library when the user chooses.
help.avoid: Avoid broad privacy promises, security guarantees, or compliance claims that have not been reviewed.

variables.label: Screens available to feature
variables.placeholder: Template picker, generation form, saved content library, export screen.
variables.hint: Describe screenshots or screens available for screenshot messages.

help.what: Optional context for the screens available to feature field.
help.why: Screenshot messages should describe real screens rather than imagined UI.
help.example: Good example: Template picker, generation form, saved content library, export screen.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Preferred search themes
variables.placeholder: AI writing, content templates, creator workflow.
variables.hint: List search themes or phrases to consider.

help.what: Optional context for the preferred search themes field.
help.why: Keyword direction helps ASO without encouraging stuffing.
help.example: Good example: AI writing, content templates, creator workflow
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Terms to avoid
variables.placeholder: best, guaranteed, viral, #1
variables.hint: List terms, competitor names, or claims to avoid.

help.what: Optional context for the terms to avoid field.
help.why: Avoid terms protect against misleading claims and keyword-policy risks.
help.example: Good example: best, guaranteed, viral, #1
help.avoid: Avoid leaving out terms that could create misleading, risky, or unsupported app-store copy.

variables.label: Promotional focus
variables.placeholder: Early Access launch positioning without fake scarcity.
variables.hint: Describe the promotion angle for promotional text.

help.what: Optional context for the promotional focus field.
help.why: Promotional copy should be based on a real focus, not invented urgency.
help.example: Good example: Early Access launch positioning without fake scarcity.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Existing listing copy
variables.placeholder: Paste the current subtitle, description, keywords, and promo text.
variables.hint: Paste current listing copy when refreshing an existing listing.

help.what: Optional context for the existing listing copy field.
help.why: Refreshes and updates should improve real existing copy rather than start from assumptions.
help.example: Good example: Paste the current subtitle, description, keywords, and promo text.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Version number
variables.hint: Enter the version number for update copy.

help.what: Optional context for the version number field.
help.why: Release notes should identify the relevant app version when supplied.
help.example: Good example: 1.2.0
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Changes in this version
variables.placeholder: Added saved prompts and improved export labels.
variables.hint: List confirmed changes, fixes, or improvements in this version.

help.what: Optional context for the changes in this version field.
help.why: Release notes must describe real changes only.
help.example: Good example: Added saved prompts and improved export labels.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Sensitive content category
variables.placeholder: Select if relevant
variables.hint: Choose a sensitive or regulated category when applicable.

help.what: Optional context for the sensitive content category field.
help.why: Sensitive categories require extra claim caution and review notes.
help.example: Good example: None
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Intended age audience
variables.placeholder: Select intended age audience
variables.hint: Choose the intended audience age group.

help.what: Optional context for the intended age audience field.
help.why: Age context affects wording, claims, privacy, and store-review considerations.
help.example: Good example: General audience
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Claims and wording restrictions
variables.placeholder: Do not claim guaranteed reach, rankings, revenue, or time savings.
variables.hint: List claims, phrases, or topics that must not appear.

help.what: Optional context for the claims and wording restrictions field.
help.why: Restrictions prevent unsafe, unsupported, or non-compliant listing language.
help.example: Good example: Do not claim guaranteed reach, rankings, revenue, or time savings.
help.avoid: Avoid leaving out terms that could create misleading, risky, or unsupported app-store copy.

variables.label: Include in the output
variables.placeholder: Select output modules
variables.hint: List the sections the generated package should include. Current form controls do not support checkboxes for this template yet, so list the relevant options separated by commas.

help.what: List one or more relevant options separated by commas. This preserves the prompt variable while using the current textarea control.
help.why: Requested modules control output size and whether screenshots, promo text, release notes, or review reports are included.
help.example: Good example: Core listing copy, Keyword suggestions, Screenshot messages, Review report
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.defaultValue: Core listing copy, Keyword suggestions, Screenshot messages, Review report

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Use creatornivo.com as the canonical domain if a URL is needed.
variables.hint: Add final constraints or context that does not fit elsewhere.

help.what: Optional context for the additional requirements field.
help.why: This captures uncommon requirements without adding extra prompt variables.
help.example: Good example: Use creatornivo.com as the canonical domain if a URL is needed.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, unsupported superlatives, keyword stuffing, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.
